summary: Jam‑heavy second set with epic Dark Star > Weather Prelude > Half‑Step
blurb: The show delivers a soaring Dark Star that segues into the Weather Report Suite Prelude and a marathon Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo. Highlights include luminous Box of Rain, Bird Song and an emotive Stella Blue. The band is tight throughout with Phil’s bass front‑and‑center.
review: On November 19, 1972 at Houston’s Hofheinz Pavilion the Dead unleashed a powerhouse two‑set marathon that still feels fresh after five decades. The first set rides a smooth groove through classics like “Bertha,” “Sugaree” and an inspired “Bird Song,” but it’s the second set that truly shines: a 20‑minute “Dark Star” rockets into the “Weather Report Suite Prelude” and rolls straight into a blistering “Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo.” That sequence, along with a soaring “Box of Rain,” a soulful “Stella Blue,” and the rare cover “Tomorrow Is Forever,” showcases Jerry’s bright, forward‑placed guitar, Phil’s hot, front‑and‑center bass, and Keith’s playful piano touches. The energy stays high through “Sugar Magnolia” into a rousing “Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad.” Across all tapes the performance is consistently praised; only a couple of recordings note minor audio quirks or mixed feelings about the Dark Star, but none detract from the overall brilliance. For anyone wanting to hear this show at its best, the crystal‑clear Miller‑Gans soundboard capture (gd1972-11-19.122784.sbd.miller-gans.flac24) delivers superb instrument separation and captures every nuance of that legendary night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Seven of nine recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the Dark Star/Weather Prelude/Half‑Step suite, Box of Rain, Bird Song and Stella Blue; only two are mixed with minor audio or performance notes, resulting in a solid positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard with excellent instrument separation; captures the full energy of the jam‑heavy second set and highlights the standout Dark Star > Weather Prelude > Half‑Step sequence.

key_highlights: Epic 20‑minute Dark Star that flows into Weather Report Suite Prelude and Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo
Lush Bird Song and soaring Box of Rain with Phil’s bass front‑and‑center
Emotive Stella Blue performance in the second set
Rare cover “Tomorrow Is Forever” adds an unexpected surprise

band_performance.Jerry: Bright, forward‑placed guitar with inventive phrasing; vocals on “Tomorrow” noted and backing vocals on B.I.O.D.T.L. praised.
band_performance.Phil: Hot, isolated bass that sits front‑and‑center; long solo in Dark Star, stellar work on Box of Rain and Stella Blue, overall tight rhythm foundation.
band_performance.Bob: Notable lyric change in “Me & My Uncle”; otherwise solid rhythm guitar supporting the jams.
band_performance.Keith: Nice, fun piano work with a brief “Stars and Stripes Forever” quote after Box of Rain; adds color without overpowering the mix.
